title: Understanding the Rocket Equation
text: To find the required exhaust speed, we can use the rocket equation, which relates the change in velocity to the exhaust speed and the mass of the rocket.

title: Rocket Equation Formula
text: The rocket equation is given by: \( \Delta v $$=$$ $$v_e$$ \cdot \ln \left( \frac{m_i}{m_f} \right) \), where \( \Delta v \) is the change in velocity, \( $$v_e$$ \) is the exhaust speed, \( $$m_i$$ \) is the initial mass, and \( $$m_f$$ \) is the final mass.

title: Calculate Change in Velocity
text: The change in velocity \( \Delta v \) is the final velocity minus the initial velocity. Calculate \( \Delta v \) using the given velocities.

title: Calculate \( \Delta v \)
text: Given the initial velocity is $$800$$ $$\frac{m}{s}$$ and the final velocity is $$1000$$ $$\frac{m}{s}$$, calculate \( \Delta v \).

title: Determine Initial and Final Mass
text: The initial mass \( $$m_i$$ \) is the total mass of the rocket, and the final mass \( $$m_f$$ \) is the mass after the fuel is used. Calculate these values.

title: Calculate Masses
text: The initial mass \( $$m_i$$ \) is $$1200$$ kg. The final mass \( $$m_f$$ \) is the initial mass minus the fuel mass. Calculate \( $$m_f$$ \).

title: Solve for Exhaust Speed
text: Rearrange the rocket equation to solve for the exhaust speed \( $$v_e$$ \).

title: Calculate Exhaust Speed
text: Using the rocket equation \( \Delta v $$=$$ $$v_e$$ \cdot \ln \left( \frac{m_i}{m_f} \right) \), solve for \( $$v_e$$ \).
